title: Graphene production method
abstract: The present invention relates to a method of producing a two-dimensional material from a three-dimensional, multi-layered material, comprising the steps of (i) intercalation of a non-ionic, volatile intercalant, (ii) drying and (iii) exfoliation of the dried material using microwaves. The invention also relates to the product formed by the method and articles comprising the product.
claims: Claims 1 . A method of producing a two-dimensional material from a three-dimensional, multi-layered material, the method comprising the steps of (i) intercalating the three-dimensional, multi-layered material with an intercalant to produce an intercalated three-dimensional, multi-layered material; (ii) drying the intercalated three-dimensional, multi-layered material to form a preactivated three-dimensional, multi-layered material; and (iii) applying microwaves for up to 1 minute to exfoliate the pre-activated three- dimensional, multi-layered material; wherein the intercalant is non-ionic and is volatile at a temperature at or below 400Â°C. 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the two-dimensional, single layer material is graphene and the three-dimensional, multi-layered material is graphite. 3. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the intercalant comprises a halogen. 4. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the intercalant comprises dibromine. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the intercalating step is carried out at room temperature. 6. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein microwaves are applied in step (iii) for up to 30 seconds. 7. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein intercalating step (i) involves applying the three-dimensional, multi-layered material to an intercalant which is in an aqueous or organic solvent, and allowing intercalation to proceed for at least 1 hour without stirring. 8. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, which comprises a step of washing the intercalated three-dimensional, multi-layered material before the drying step to remove excess intercalant. 9. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising a further step (iv) which involves dispersing the exfoliated three-dimensional, multi-layered material in a liquid. 10. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein intercalating step (i) is carried out under vacuum. 1 1 . A method according to any one of the preceding claims, which includes a step of re-using the intercalant to produce further two-dimensional material from further three-dimensional, multi-layered material. 12 An article produced by a method according to any one of the preceding claims. 13. An article according to claim 12, produced by a method which consists of steps (i)-(iii), wherein the article has 2-5 layers of material and is defect free. 14. An article comprising a two-dimensional material, comprising applying the material produced according to any one of the preceding claims to a polymer or glass substrate.
